page: Microsoft Azure D4a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the DAv4 family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, which translates to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance includes 102 GB of local storage, does not feature any GPU accelerators, and is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance registers a strong performance in the top 25% with a score of 2704.79. However, multi-core performance is weaker, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is mixed; read-only small block cached bandwidth is weak at 259.03 GB/sec, while large block uncached read bandwidth is average at 40.46 GB/sec. Large block write bandwidth is weak at 24.31 GB/sec. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance, whereas multi-threaded bzip3 decompression drops to the bottom 10%.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the D4a_v4 offers a standard entry-level hardware profile. The presence of 102 GB of local storage provides a baseline for local caching and temporary files. However, the weak multi-core scaling, lower database operations score of 1849.52 in PassMark, and low Redis SET throughput of 3.95 million operations per second indicate that this instance is not optimized for heavy multi-threaded database workloads or high-throughput web serving. It is best utilized for general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, lightweight text processing, and basic development or testing environments where single-core performance is prioritized over multi-core density.
description: Microsoft Azure D4a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks demonstrate strong single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. However, multi-core scaling is limited, with multi-core CPU and memory write benchmarks falling into the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the instance suitable for general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, and lightweight text processing, rather than high-throughput database operations or multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4a_v4 is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage, offering strong single-core but weaker multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4a_v4 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, 102 GB local storage, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, strong single-core performance, and integrated local storage for standard workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM.
Includes 102 GB of local storage for temporary files and caching.
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks.
Exhibits weaker multi-core scaling and lower memory write bandwidth.
Best suited for general-purpose computing, single-threaded applications, and development environments.
